title: astragalus rostratus
intro: Astragalus rostratus, also known as the beaked milk vetch, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus rostratus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and improve cardiovascular health. Astragalus rostratus is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, or tinctures, which are often consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
